_comment: WC2026 Group H, MD3. RECALIBRATED with team news (~confirmed/predicted XIs). Cape Verde: Sidny Lopes SUSPENDED (2nd yellow vs Uruguay) -> loses a starting defender (weaker at the back); Jovane Cabral (knock) and Telmo Arcanjo (hamstring) DOUBTFUL -> attack slightly trimmed. Saudi: full squad, Mohamed Kanno RETURNS to the midfield that was overrun 4-0 by Spain -> more solid/competitive. Net: the gap NARROWS (Cape Verde a touch down, Saudi a touch up) -> a more even, open must-win for both. Modest adjustment only (lesson: adjust for confirmed team news, not narrative). Home = Cape Verde, away = Saudi Arabia. Referee/cards not modelled.

meta.stakes: Both need a win to qualify (Cape Verde for 2nd/best-third, Saudi for a slim chance). Cape Verde missing suspended DF Sidny Lopes (and Jovane Cabral doubtful); Saudi get Kanno back to steady the midfield. A more even, open game than the pre-XI read.

home.name: Cape Verde
notes: CONFIRMED 4-2-3-1, lone striker (Livramento); without Sidny Lopes (susp.), Jovane Cabral AND Garry Rodrigues -> modest attack. attack 1.00.
Reshuffled back line without Sidny Lopes. defense 1.02.

away.name: Saudi Arabia
notes: CONFIRMED 4-4-2 with TWO strikers (Mandash + Al-Buraikan) and Kanno steadying midfield -> more attacking than the lone-striker read. attack 1.05.
Cape Verde missing defenders helps. defense 1.05.

_lineups.Cape Verde: 4-2-3-1: Vozinha; Paulo, Pico, Borges, Pina; Duarte, Lenini; Semedo, Monteiro, Mendes; Livramento. WITHOUT Sidny Lopes (suspended), Jovane Cabral AND Garry Rodrigues -> modest attack, lone striker.
_lineups.Saudi Arabia: 4-4-2: Al-Owais; N.Al-Dawsari, Tambakti, Al-Amri, Boushal; S.Al-Dawsari, Kanno, Al-Khaibari, Abdulhamid; Mandash, Al-Buraikan. Kanno starts; TWO strikers -> more attacking.
_lineups._note: Confirmed XIs nudge the game to near-even: Cape Verde attack a touch weaker (no Garry Rodrigues, lone striker), Saudi a touch more attacking (4-4-2, two forwards).

_postmatch.result: Cape Verde 0-0 Saudi Arabia. Goalless; both depleted attacks cancelled out.
_postmatch.verdict: Flagged as a near-even coin-flip with NO strong read; the draw (28.4%) came in -- the tightest version, 0-0. The CONFIRMED-XI recalibration (both attacks depleted -> lower-scoring, more even) was directionally RIGHT: I cut Cape Verde with the lone-striker XI and the game was indeed low-scoring (0 goals, Under 1.5 which was only 28.7%). No decisive 1X2 winner because there genuinely wasn't a favourite. Cape Verde still qualify 2nd; the draw was enough.
_postmatch.lesson: On a true coin-flip, grade the texture: the 'even + low-scoring' read (post-XI) was right; the 1X2 had no edge to be right about. A depleted must-win can cancel into 0-0.
